title: Learn Dublin Area Knowledge lesson by lesson.
description: The first 100 of 413 Dublin Area questions, structured by question pattern and geography to improve recall.

lessons.title: One-Way Streets
lessons.intro: Learn one-way directions as fixed start-to-end street pairs.
lessons.memoryTrick: Say every answer as an arrow: FROM → TO. Reverse order means wrong.
commonMistakes: Remembering both street names but reversing the direction
Choosing the street pair from the map without checking the one-way direction

lessons.title: Roads, Routes and Connections
lessons.intro: Study which areas roads connect, major routes, national roads and crossings.
lessons.memoryTrick: Build a chain: AREA → ROAD → AREA. For national routes, remember the road number as the middle link.
commonMistakes: Choosing two nearby areas that the road does not directly connect
Confusing a local road with the main national route

lessons.title: Hotels, Hospitals, Colleges and Major Venues
lessons.intro: Group practical taxi destinations by venue type and the street or district where each is located.
lessons.memoryTrick: Use destination pairs: VENUE = STREET/AREA. Picture the drop-off point, not just the wider district.
commonMistakes: Selecting a nearby road instead of the venue's actual address
Confusing similarly named schools, hospitals or hotels

lessons.title: Landmarks, Churches, Parks and Entertainment
lessons.intro: Memorise cultural venues, pubs, churches, parks, theatres and civic landmarks.
lessons.memoryTrick: Attach each landmark to one anchor street and one nearby famous place.
commonMistakes: Confusing venue names with the district they are in

lessons.title: North Dublin Area Knowledge
lessons.intro: Learn Northside districts, connecting roads and key destinations together as geographic clusters.
lessons.memoryTrick: Move outward from the city: Marino/Clontarf → Raheny/Killester → Howth/Baldoyle/Portmarnock.
commonMistakes: Confusing the road serving a venue with the wider area

lessons.title: South and West Dublin Area Knowledge
lessons.intro: Study Southside and West Dublin roads, suburbs, institutions and routes in connected groups.
lessons.memoryTrick: Use corridor memory: coast, N11 corridor, south-west suburbs, then N4/Lucan corridor.
commonMistakes: Mixing Blackrock, Stillorgan and Dundrum roads
Confusing south-city streets with suburban roads

lessons.title: Areas, Boundaries and Location Logic
lessons.intro: Practise which areas surround, include or exclude a location, plus general street-location questions.
lessons.memoryTrick: Use elimination: identify three places that clearly fit the same geographic cluster; the outsider is the answer.
commonMistakes: Choosing the familiar place rather than checking geographic fit
Ignoring words such as NOT, closest, mostly or directly

lessons.title: Final Mixed Dublin Area Test
lessons.intro: All currently loaded Dublin Area questions mixed together.
lessons.memoryTrick: First identify the question pattern: direction, connection, venue, area or route.
commonMistakes: Answering before identifying the question pattern
Treating nearby locations as exact addresses

questions.questionText: What two areas does the Rock Road connect?
questions.explanation: The correct answer was taken from the green highlight or right-tick shown in the supplied question source. Red-cross selections were treated as incorrect.
questions.memoryTip: Create a three-part chain: place, connecting road, destination.
